title: Broken Access Control

quiz.question: What is IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)?
options: A) A type of SQL injection
B) Accessing resources by manipulating object identifiers without authorization checks
quiz.answer: B) Accessing resources by manipulating object identifiers without authorization checks
quiz.explanation: IDOR occurs when applications use user-supplied input to access objects directly without verifying ownership or permissions.

quiz.question: What is the difference between vertical and horizontal privilege escalation?
options: A) Vertical is user-to-admin, horizontal is user-to-user
B) Vertical is network-level, horizontal is application-level
C) Vertical uses SQL injection, horizontal uses XSS
quiz.answer: A) Vertical is user-to-admin, horizontal is user-to-user
quiz.explanation: Vertical escalation gains higher privileges (e.g., user to admin), while horizontal escalation accesses resources of other users at the same privilege level.

quiz.question: Which CORS configuration is most secure?
options: A) Allow all origins with wildcard (*)
quiz.explanation: Whitelisting specific origins ensures only trusted domains can access your API, preventing unauthorized cross-origin requests.
